Can any pagan god raise up someone in righteousness, make use of them as he pleases, and give them victory over nations? The Lord did so with Abraham, or rather, he would do so with Cyrus. Sinners encourage one another in sinful ways; should not the servants of the living God stir one another up in his service? God’s people are the seed of Abraham, his friend. This is certainly the highest title ever given to a mortal. It means that Abraham, by Divine grace, was made like to God, and he was admitted into fellowship with Him. Happy are the servants of the Lord, whom he has called to be his friends, and to walk with him in faith and holy obedience. Let those who have been favored thus not yield to fear; for the contest may be sharp, but the victory will be sure.

God speaks tenderly: Fear not, for I am with you—not only within reach, but present with you. Are you weak? I will strengthen you. Are you in need of friends? I will help you in time of need. Are you ready to fall? I will uphold you with that right hand which is full of righteousness, dealing out rewards and punishments. There are those who strive against God’s people, seeking their ruin. Let God’s people not render evil for evil, but wait for God's time. He is like the worm Jacob—so small, so weak, so despised and trampled on by everyone. God’s people are as worms, in humble thoughts of themselves, and in their enemies’ haughty thoughts of them; worms, but not vipers, not of the serpent’s seed. Every part of God's word is designed to humble human pride and make one appear small in his own eyes. The Lord will help them, for he is their Redeemer. The Lord will make Jacob become a threshing instrument. God will make him fit for use, new, and with sharp spikes. This has fulfillment in the triumphs of the gospel of Christ, and of all faithful followers of Christ, over the power of darkness. God has provided comforts to supply all their needs and answer all their prayers. Our way to heaven lies through the wilderness of this world. The soul of man is in need and seeks satisfaction; but becomes weary of seeking that in the world, which cannot be found there. Yet they will have a constant supply where one would least expect it. I will open rivers of grace, rivers of living water, which Christ spoke of the Spirit. When God sets up his church in the Gentile wilderness, there will be a great change, as if thorns and briers were turned into cedars, fir trees, and myrtles. These blessings are kept for the poor in spirit, who long for Divine enlightenment, pardon, and holiness. And God will render their barren souls fruitful in the grace of his Spirit, so that all who behold may consider it.

There needs no more to show the folly of sin than to bring notice to the reasons given in defense of it. There is nothing in idols worthy of regard; they are less than nothing and worse than nothing. Let those who advocate doctrines other than salvation through Christ, bring their arguments. Can they tell of a cure for human depravity? Jehovah has power which cannot be withstood; he will make this appear. But the certain knowledge of the future must be only with Jehovah, who fulfills his own plans. All prophecies, except those of the Bible, have been uncertain. In the work of redemption the Lord showed himself much more than in the release of the Jews from Babylon. The good news the Lord will send in the gospel is a mystery hidden from ages and generations. A Deliverer has been raised up for us, of nobler name and greater power than the deliverer of the captive Jews. May we be numbered among his obedient servants and faithful friends.
